perturbation.original_text: PACIFICAP ENTERTAINMENT will pay any and all costs, damages, and expenses, including, but not limited to, reasonable attorneys' fees and costs awarded against or otherwise incurred by Michael Henry, Melba Henry, THE HENRY FILM AND ENTERTAINMENT CORPORATION or any employees, representatives, agents and agent affiliates in connection with or arising from any such claim, suit, action or proceeding.
perturbation.changed_text: PACIFICAP ENTERTAINMENT will pay 50% of all costs, damages, and expenses, including reasonable attorneys' fees and costs awarded against or otherwise incurred by Michael Henry, Melba Henry, THE HENRY FILM AND ENTERTAINMENT CORPORATION or any employees, representatives, agents and agent affiliates in connection with or arising from any such claim, suit, action or proceeding, up to a maximum of $10,000 per claim.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states PACIFICAP ENTERTAINMENT pays *all* costs related to indemnification. The modified text limits PACIFICAP ENTERTAINMENT's payment to 50% of the costs up to a maximum of $10,000 per claim. This creates an in-text contradiction in the indemnification terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: INDEMNIFICATION PACIFICAP ENTERTAINMENT, at its own expense, will indemnify, defend and hold harmless Michael Henry, THE HENRY FILM AND ENTERTAINMENT CORPORATION, its Affiliates and their employees, representatives, agents and agent affiliates, against any claim, suit, action, or other proceeding brought against THE HENRY FILM AND ENTERTAINMENT CORPORATION, or an Affiliate based on or arising from any claim that PACIFICAP ENTERTAINMENT Content as delivered to THE HENRY FILM AND ENTERTAINMENT CORPORATION or any PACIFICAP ENTERTAINMENT Brand Feature infringes in any manner any third party Ownership Rights or Ownership Issues, Intellectual Property Right of any third party or contains any material or information that is defamatory, libelous, slanderous, that violates any person's right of publicity, privacy or personality, or has otherwise resulted in any injury, damage or harm to any person; provided, however, that in any such case: (x) THE HENRY FILM AND ENTERTAINMENT CORPORATION provides PACIFICAP ENTERTAINMENT with prompt notice of any such claim; (y) PACIFICAP ENTERTAINMENT permits THE HENRY FILM AND ENTERTAINMENT CORPORATION. . to assume and control the defense of such action, with counsel chosen by PACIFICAP ENTERTAINMENT (who shall be reasonably acceptable to THE HENRY FILM AND ENTERTAINMENT CORPORATION. .); and (z) THE HENRY FILM AND ENTERTAINMENT CORPORATION does not enter into any settlement or compromise of any such claim without PACIFICAP ENTERTAINMENT'S prior written consent. PACIFICAP ENTERTAINMENT will pay 50% of all costs, damages, and expenses, including reasonable attorneys' fees and costs awarded against or otherwise incurred by Michael Henry, Melba Henry, THE HENRY FILM AND ENTERTAINMENT CORPORATION or any employees, representatives, agents and agent affiliates in connection with or arising from any such claim, suit, action or proceeding, up to a maximum of $10,000 per claim.
perturbation.justification: The original text in the 'INDEMNIFICATION' section implied full coverage by PACIFICAP ENTERTAINMENT. The changed text limits PACIFICAP ENTERTAINMENT's financial responsibility to 50% of costs up to $10,000 per claim. This creates a direct contradiction as the indemnification obligation is no longer absolute, impacting the scope of protection offered in the 'INDEMNIFICATION' section.

perturbation.original_text: After the Initial Term, this Agreement will be automatically renewed for successive additional 3~year periods ("Extension Terms"), unless otherwise terminated by either party by giving notice to the other party not less than sixty (60) days prior to the end of a Term.
perturbation.changed_text: After the Initial Term, this Agreement will be automatically renewed for successive additional 3~year periods ("Extension Terms"), unless otherwise terminated by either party by giving notice to the other party not less than thirty (30) days prior to the end of a Term.
perturbation.explanation: The original text requires 60 days' notice for termination before the end of a term. The modification changes this to 30 days' notice. This creates an in-text contradiction concerning the required notice period for termination during extension terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: INITIAL TERM AND RENEWALS: This Agreement will become effective as of the last date of signature (Effective Date) and shall, unless sooner terminated as provided below or as otherwise agreed, remain effective for an initial term of 10 Years following the first date of public availability of the PACIFICAP ENTERTAINMENT Content within a THE HENRY FILM AND ENTERTAINMENT CORPORATION Property (the "Initial Term"). After the Initial Term, this Agreement will be automatically renewed for successive additional 3~year periods ("Extension Terms"), unless otherwise terminated by either party by giving notice to the other party not less than thirty (30) days prior to the end of a Term. As used herein, the "Term" means the Initial Term and any Extension Term(s).
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the notice period for termination from 60 days to 30 days. The contradicted text, which may or may not be in the same section (but is in this case), also states that the agreement can be terminated with 30 days' notice. While the changed text and contradicted text are now aligned, the *explanation* explicitly states that the change *creates* an in-text contradiction because the original text required 60 days. The change creates a contradiction with what *was* previously stated in the contradicted text. The original text required a 60-day notice, and the modified text requires a 30-day notice, thus conflicting with the initial 60-day requirement and now creating uncertainty around how the agreement can be terminated.
